title: Dryopteris catoptera

The family Dryopteridaceae is a group of ferns that are characterized by their pinnate or bipinnate leaves. Dryopteris catoptera is native to certain regions, although specific information on its native range is not readily available. Further research is needed to confirm its classification and distribution.
uses: Dryopteris catoptera, also known as the Chinese wood fern, has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The plant's rhizome is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and infections.
